methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 49.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Fergani Space is a private-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Selçuk Bayraktar. The valuation universe comprises 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace.

Standard triangulation methods face material constraints. Peer-median anchoring is inapplicable: while 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, filtering for stage, adjacency, or valuation tier yields fewer than 3 datapoints in any narrow cohort, making a representative median calculation impossible. Public-comp anchoring is similarly blocked because Fergani Space's revenue is not disclosed—a common limitation for private companies—precluding application of the sector's 49.8x P/S median (calculated across the 6 public tickers).

This analysis cannot support a specific valuation range without either disclosed financials or a sufficiently dense private peer dataset at similar stage and scale. Key uncertainties include Fergani's revenue run-rate, profitability profile, and operational maturity relative to the identified private peer set. Resolution of these data gaps—particularly revenue disclosure or comparable transaction data from the private frontier-space segment—is necessary to anchor a defensible valuation view.
